fact_template: The shortest war in history lasted only _______ minutes.
A group of flamingos is called a _______.
Honey never spoils — archaeologists have found 3000-year-old honey in Egyptian tombs that was still _______.
The Eiffel Tower can grow up to _______ centimetres taller in summer due to thermal expansion.
Scotland's national animal is the _______.
A day on _______ is longer than a year on it.
The average person walks the equivalent of _______ times around the Earth in a lifetime.
Cleopatra lived closer in time to the Moon landing than to the construction of the _______.
The inventor of the Pringles can is buried inside one — his ashes were placed in a _______ container.
Wombats are the only animals in the world that produce _______ shaped droppings.
The full name of Los Angeles is El Pueblo de Nuestra Señora la Reina de los Ángeles del Río de _______.
Octopuses have _______ hearts.
The word 'nerd' was first used by Dr. Seuss in his 1950 book _______.
A bolt of lightning is five times hotter than the surface of the _______.
The country with the most natural lakes in the world is _______.
Bananas are technically _______ from a botanical standpoint.
The longest English word you can type using only the top row of a keyboard is _______.
Crows can recognize and remember human _______ for years.
The Great Wall of China is NOT visible from _______ with the naked eye.
Nintendo was founded in _______ and originally sold playing cards.
The Oxford English Dictionary added the word _______ in 2001, making it the first emoji-inspired word.
A group of owls is called a _______.
The average cloud weighs about _______ pounds.
Humans share _______ percent of their DNA with bananas.
The man who invented _______ was afraid of the dark.
A snail can sleep for up to _______ years.
The shortest complete sentence in the English language is _______.
Polar bear fur is actually _______ in color, not white.
The human nose can detect over _______ trillion different scents.
The country of _______ has more pyramids than Egypt.
A jiffy is an actual unit of time equal to _______ of a second.
The first product to have a _______ scanned was a pack of Wrigley's chewing gum in 1974.
Lobsters were once so plentiful they were used as _______ in colonial America.
The leg bones of a bat are so thin that no bat can _______ from a standing position.
The first recorded use of the word 'hello' as a greeting dates to _______ when the telephone was invented.
There are more possible iterations of a game of chess than there are _______ in the observable universe.
The world's oldest known recipe is for _______.
A blue whale's heart is the size of a small _______.
The sentence 'The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog' uses every letter of the alphabet — this is called a _______.
Butterflies taste with their _______.
The Great Fire of London in 1666 destroyed most of the city but killed only _______ people.
Technically, _______ is not a color — it's the absence of all color.
The tongue of a blue whale weighs as much as an adult _______.
The dot over the letters 'i' and 'j' is called a _______.
Oxford University is older than the _______ Empire.
The only letter that doesn't appear in any U.S. state name is _______.
Ketchup was sold as a _______ in the 1830s.
The plastic tip at the end of a shoelace is called an _______.
The world's largest desert is _______, not the Sahara.
Sharks are older than _______ — they existed before trees evolved.
The first _______ was invented in 1908 and was made of silk.
In space, you cannot _______ because there's no air to create a sound wave.
The average person produces enough _______ in their lifetime to fill two swimming pools.
Peanuts are not actually _______ — they are legumes.
The fear of long words is called _______.
Alaska is simultaneously the most northern, most western, AND most _______ state in the US.
The smell of rain is called _______.
Sloths can hold their breath longer than _______, up to 40 minutes.
Marie Curie's notebooks are still too _______ to handle safely without protection.
The original name for the search engine Google was _______.
Cats have a dedicated _______ meow they use only to communicate with humans.
The first item ever sold on eBay was a broken _______ for $14.83.
The shortest war ever fought was between _______ and Zanzibar.
Figs are not technically a _______ — they are an inverted flower cluster.
Starfish don't have a _______— they absorb oxygen directly through their skin.
The number of possible shuffles of a deck of 52 cards is greater than the number of _______ in the universe.
The first known _______ game was played in China around 2300 BC.
The city of _______ has more canals than Venice.
Humans are the only animals that blush — this is caused by a rush of _______ to the face.
The Mona Lisa has no visible _______ or eyelashes.
Coca-Cola was originally green because of the color of the _______ used in the original recipe.
The Hawaiian alphabet has only _______ letters.
Horned lizards can squirt _______ from their eyes as a defense mechanism.
The average person will spend six months of their life waiting for _______ to turn green.
Wyoming and _______ are the only two US states that are perfect rectangles.
The world record for the most _______ eaten in one minute is 9.
Cows have best friends and become _______ when separated from them.
The inventor of the World Wide Web, Tim Berners-Lee, originally called it _______.
The first computer bug was an actual _______ found in a Harvard computer in 1947.
In Switzerland it is illegal to own just one _______ because they are so social.
The average person walks about _______ steps per day.
Mount Everest is not the closest point to outer space — that honor goes to Mount _______.
A group of cats is called a _______.
The word 'salary' comes from the Latin word for _______ because Roman soldiers were paid in it.
Russia has a larger land area than the entire surface of _______.
The first microwave oven was called the _______ Radarange.
Elephants are the only animals that cannot _______.
The tongue print of every human is as unique as their _______.
The longest word in the English language with no repeated letters is _______.
The country with the longest coastline in the world is _______.
The average lightning bolt is only about _______ inches wide.
Cleopatra lived closer in time to the _______ than to the construction of the Great Pyramid.
Mosquitoes are attracted to people who have recently eaten _______.
The odds of being killed by a vending machine are higher than the odds of being killed by a _______.
The first known novel ever written was 'The Tale of Genji,' written in Japan around the year _______ by a woman.
In Japan, letting a sumo wrestler make your baby _______ is considered good luck.
The most expensive spice in the world by weight is _______.
A group of porcupines is called a _______.
The Space Shuttle's engines were shut down _______ seconds before landing.
Competitive eating champion Takeru Kobayashi once ate _______ hot dogs in 10 minutes.
